1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbldk_):
20	#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
21	# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
22	# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch push),
23	# the substitutions blob, and the per-member envelope extract loop — and composes
24	# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
25	# step-composition machinery lives here.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	# Capture-file extension for buf_write_fact_multi (filesystem-as-data-bus).
30	# Pure literal tinder — one provenance fact-file per captured Lode, keyed by
31	# stamp: "<stamp>.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}". Mirrors the roster fact precedent.
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)
35	
36	# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch
37	# push) and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
38	# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
39	# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
40	# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
41	# Args: token origin stamp
42	zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
43	  zrbld_sentinel
44	
45	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
46	  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
47	  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"
48	
49	  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
50	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
51	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
52	
53	  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
54	  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
55	  local -r z_recipe=(
56	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}|ensconce-capture|bash"
57	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|assemble-push-vouch|bash"
58	  )
59	
60	  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
61	  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
62	  jq -n \
63	    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
64	    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
65	    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
66	    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
67	    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
68	    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
69	    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
70	    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
71	    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
72	    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
73	    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
74	    '{
75	      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
76	      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
77	      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
78	      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
79	      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
80	      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
81	      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
82	      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
83	      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
84	      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
85	      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
86	      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
87	      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
88	      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
89	      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
90	    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
91	    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"
92	
93	  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
94	    "${z_token}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
95	    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
96	    "${z_recipe[@]}"
97	}
98	
99	# Internal: extract per-Lode provenance envelopes from the completed ensconce
100	# build and write one capture-file per Lode member. The skopeo capture step
101	# (step 0) authors the base64 JSON; the docker vouch step writes no output. The
102	# spine decodes step 0's slot to a file; the per-member slot_n envelope below is
103	# bole's shape, not the spine's.
104	zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
105	  zrbld_sentinel
106	
107	  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"
108	
109	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
110	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
111	
112	  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
113	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"
114	
115	  local z_n=""
116	  local z_slot_key=""
117	  local z_stamp_file=""
118	  local z_stamp=""
119	  local z_vouch_file=""
120	  local z_vouch=""
121	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
122	    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
123	    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
124	    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
125	      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
126	    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
127	    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue
128	
129	    z_vouch_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_vouch.json"
130	    jq -c ".${z_slot_key}.vouch" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vouch_file}" \
131	      || buc_die "Failed to extract vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
132	    z_vouch=$(<"${z_vouch_file}")
133	    test -n "${z_vouch}" || buc_die "Empty vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
134	
135	    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_stamp}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}" "${z_vouch}"
136	    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — capture-file ${z_stamp}.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}"
137	  done
138	}
139	
140	######################################################################
141	# External Functions (rbld_*)
142	
143	rbld_ensconce() {
144	  zrbld_sentinel
145	
146	  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
147	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
148	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
149	
150	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
151	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
152	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
153	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
154	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
155	
156	  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
157	  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
158	  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
159	  local z_origin=""
160	  local z_origin_count=0
161	  local z_n=""
162	  local z_origin_var=""
163	  local z_slot_origin=""
164	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
165	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
166	    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
167	    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
168	    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
169	    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
170	  done
171	
172	  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
173	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
174	  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
175	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"
176	
177	  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
178	  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
179	  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
180	    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"
181	
182	  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"
183	
184	  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (skopeo for capture, docker for vouch).
185	  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images
186	
187	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
188	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
189	
190	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
191	  local z_token=""
192	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
193	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
194	
195	  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
196	  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
197	  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
198	  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
199	
200	  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
201	  zrbld_ensconce_extract
202	
203	  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
204	}
205	
206	# eof
207

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbldk_):
#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch push),
# the substitutions blob, and the per-member envelope extract loop — and composes
# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
# step-composition machinery lives here.

set -euo pipefail

# Capture-file extension for buf_write_fact_multi (filesystem-as-data-bus).
# Pure literal tinder — one provenance fact-file per captured Lode, keyed by
# stamp: "<stamp>.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}". Mirrors the roster fact precedent.

######################################################################
# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)

# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (skopeo capture + docker vouch
# push) and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
# Args: token origin stamp
zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"

  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
  local -r z_recipe=(
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}|ensconce-capture|bash"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|assemble-push-vouch|bash"
  )

  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
    '{
      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"

  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
    "${z_token}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
    "${z_recipe[@]}"
}

# Internal: extract per-Lode provenance envelopes from the completed ensconce
# build and write one capture-file per Lode member. The skopeo capture step
# (step 0) authors the base64 JSON; the docker vouch step writes no output. The
# spine decodes step 0's slot to a file; the per-member slot_n envelope below is
# bole's shape, not the spine's.
zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"

  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"

  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

  local z_n=""
  local z_slot_key=""
  local z_stamp_file=""
  local z_stamp=""
  local z_vouch_file=""
  local z_vouch=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue

    z_vouch_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_vouch.json"
    jq -c ".${z_slot_key}.vouch" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vouch_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to extract vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"
    z_vouch=$(<"${z_vouch_file}")
    test -n "${z_vouch}" || buc_die "Empty vouch envelope for ${z_slot_key}"

    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_stamp}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}" "${z_vouch}"
    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — capture-file ${z_stamp}.${RBCC_fact_ext_lode}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbld_*)

rbld_ensconce() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
  local z_origin=""
  local z_origin_count=0
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_slot_origin=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
  done

  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"

  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"

  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (skopeo for capture, docker for vouch).
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
  zrbld_ensconce_extract

  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}

# eof

lastPrompt: mount BX

content.text: The mechanism is now clear: the spine inlines each step as a **whole `.sh` file** (`zrbfc_write_script_body` → shebang + body → Cloud Build `script` field). There is **no sub-step snippet/include mechanism yet** — that's what "author as composed snippets" must build. Let me confirm `write_script_body` does no include-processing, and read the skopeo capture step plus the verify pipeline and the RBSCJ contract.

input.command: echo "=== zrbfc_write_script_body ===" && grep -n "zrbfc_write_script_body" Tools/rbk/rbfcb_BuildHost.sh | head; echo "---" && awk '/zrbfc_write_script_body\(\)/{f=1} f{print} f&&/^}/{exit}' Tools/rbk/rbfcb_BuildHost.sh
input.description: Read write_script_body implementation

content.content:
=== zrbfc_write_script_body ===
206:zrbfc_write_script_body() {
232:# and BURD_OSTYPE — so, like zrbfc_write_script_body, it carries no sentinel and
---
zrbfc_write_script_body() {
  local -r z_src="$1"
  local -r z_dst="$2"
  local z_line
  local z_seen_shebang=""
  test -r "${z_src}" || return 1
  : > "${z_dst}"     || return 1
  while IFS= read -r z_line || [ -n "${z_line}" ]; do
    if [ -z "${z_seen_shebang}" ]; then
      z_seen_shebang=1
      continue
    fi
    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" >> "${z_dst}"
  done < "${z_src}"
  return 0
}

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via skopeo
3	# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
6	#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
7	#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
8	#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
9	#
10	# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: inspect upstream, measure the canonical
11	# digest, copy --all into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the member
12	# tags by GAR->GAR retag (dedups blobs). Author the provenance envelope and stage
13	# it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
14	# /builder/outputs/output. Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server.
15	#
16	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
17	# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
18	#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
19	#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
20	#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
21	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="
25	
26	# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA)
27	echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
28	TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
29	  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
30	  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }
31	
32	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
33	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
34	
35	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
36	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
37	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
38	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
39	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
40	SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
41	SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
42	SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"
43	
44	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
45	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
46	
47	# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
48	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
49	
50	# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
51	RESULT='{'
52	FIRST=true
53	
54	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
55	  case "${SLOT}" in
56	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
57	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
58	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
59	  esac
60	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
61	  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }
62	
63	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"
64	
65	  # Inspect upstream for raw manifest (manifest list or single manifest).
66	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
67	  skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
68	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
69	
70	  # Canonical digest = sha256 of the raw manifest (matches what every tool reports).
71	  # sha256sum (coreutils) — this runs inside the skopeo reliquary container.
72	  SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
73	  test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
74	
75	  # Sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars (legacy anchor form).
76	  SANITIZED=$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')
77	  SHORT="${SHA:0:10}"
78	  FINGERPRINT_TAG="${SANITIZED}-${SHORT}"
79	
80	  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
81	  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
82	
83	  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
84	  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
85	  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT_TAG}"
86	
87	  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag.
88	  skopeo copy --all \
89	    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
90	    "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
91	    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
92	    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
93	
94	  # Apply remaining member tags by GAR->GAR retag (same blobs, manifest re-tag only).
95	  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT_TAG}"; do
96	    skopeo copy --all \
97	      "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
98	      "docker://${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}" \
99	      --src-creds  "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
100	      --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
101	      || { echo "FATAL: skopeo retag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
102	  done
103	
104	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"
105	
106	  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
107	  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
108	  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
109	  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
110	  ENVELOPE='{'
111	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
112	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
113	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
114	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
115	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
116	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
117	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
118	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
119	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via skopeo
# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
#
# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: inspect upstream, measure the canonical
# digest, copy --all into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the member
# tags by GAR->GAR retag (dedups blobs). Author the provenance envelope and stage
# it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
# /builder/outputs/output. Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server.
#
# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="

# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA)
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"

# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"

  # Inspect upstream for raw manifest (manifest list or single manifest).
  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
  skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Canonical digest = sha256 of the raw manifest (matches what every tool reports).
  # sha256sum (coreutils) — this runs inside the skopeo reliquary container.
  SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
  test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars (legacy anchor form).
  SANITIZED=$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')
  SHORT="${SHA:0:10}"
  FINGERPRINT_TAG="${SANITIZED}-${SHORT}"

  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"

  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT_TAG}"

  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag.
  skopeo copy --all \
    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
    "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Apply remaining member tags by GAR->GAR retag (same blobs, manifest re-tag only).
  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT_TAG}"; do
    skopeo copy --all \
      "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
      "docker://${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}" \
      --src-creds  "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
      --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
      || { echo "FATAL: skopeo retag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
  done

  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"

  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
  ENVELOPE='{'
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT_TAG}\"]"
  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"

  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="

2	# RBGJV Step 01: Prepare verification keys
3	# Builder: alpine (from reliquary)
4	# Entrypoint: sh (not bash — alpine does not have bash)
5	# Substitutions: _RBGV_VESSEL_MODE

15	-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----

17	qc3oCcFVH3QJ37IBLUv/MUApbnNHFfD75ayJ/a0F45xa+MLv5zoep+GxsA==
18	-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
19	KEYEOF
20	  echo "Attestor public keys written to /workspace/keys/"
21	fi

#!/bin/sh
# RBGJV Step 01: Prepare verification keys
# Builder: alpine (from reliquary)
# Entrypoint: sh (not bash — alpine does not have bash)
# Substitutions: _RBGV_VESSEL_MODE

set -eu
echo "=== Prepare verification keys ==="

# Conjure-only: write GCB attestor public key for DSSE envelope verification
if [ "${_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE}" = "rbnve_conjure" ]; then
  mkdir -p /workspace/keys
  # KMS: projects/verified-builder/locations/global/keyRings/attestor/cryptoKeys/google-hosted-worker/cryptoKeyVersions/1
  cat > /workspace/keys/google-hosted-worker.pub << 'KEYEOF'
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
MFkwEwYHKoZIzj0CAQYIKoZIzj0DAQcDQgAEg9KII7kzr/30HBluf00y9WwtMFkE
qc3oCcFVH3QJ37IBLUv/MUApbnNHFfD75ayJ/a0F45xa+MLv5zoep+GxsA==
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
KEYEOF
  echo "Attestor public keys written to /workspace/keys/"
fi

content.text:
Now I have the mechanism fully characterized. The decisive finding: **the spine inlines whole `.sh` files — there is no sub-step include mechanism.** So "composed snippets" require new host-side text-assembly machinery, and "snippets read shell vars" means a snippet is a text fragment that reads shell vars the kind-specific code sets *before the fragment runs in the build container* (capturing `_RBGx_` substitutions into agreed names first) — which is exactly what makes one snippet serve two disjoint substitution namespaces.

Let me read the RBSCJ contract the spine pace established, and confirm the shared blocks against enshrine (reference, not migrated) so I author the snippet boundary correctly.

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJE Step 01: Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via skopeo
# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGE_GAR_HOST, _RBGE_GAR_PATH, _RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT,
#                _RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY,
#                _RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN
#
# For each non-empty ORIGIN slot: inspect upstream manifest, compute anchor,
# skopeo copy --all to GAR. Write JSON locator results to /builder/outputs/output.
# Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server for GAR destination.
#
# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<ENSHRINES_ROOT>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
# Locator shape (written back to rbrv.env): <ENSHRINES_CATEGORY>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
# Anchor as both path segment and tag — each anchor is a peer package under
# the enshrines namespace, not a tag on a shared package.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Enshrine base images to GAR ==="

# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA)
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"

# Initialize result JSON
RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}" ;;
    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}" ;;
    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue

  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} ---"

  # Inspect upstream for raw manifest (manifest list or single manifest)
  RAW_FILE="/workspace/enshrine_raw_${SLOT}.json"
  skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Compute sha256 of the raw manifest
  # Note: sha256sum (coreutils), not openssl — this runs inside the skopeo reliquary container
  SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
  test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Construct anchor: sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars
  SANITIZED=$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')
  SHORT="${SHA:0:10}"
  ANCHOR="${SANITIZED}-${SHORT}"

  # Compose locator (package-path:tag, no cloud prefix — caller prepends at use-site)
  LOCATOR="${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"

  echo "Anchor: ${ANCHOR}"
  echo "Locator: ${LOCATOR}"
  echo "Digest: sha256:${SHA}"

  # Construct GAR destination — anchor is both the package name and the tag
  DEST_REF="${_RBGE_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGE_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"
  echo "Dest: ${DEST_REF}"

  # Copy upstream to GAR with anchor tag, preserving manifest list
  skopeo copy --all \
    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
    "docker://${DEST_REF}" \
    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "Slot ${SLOT} enshrined: ${ANCHOR}"

  # Accumulate JSON result (no jq dependency — printf safe for sanitized values)
  # The "anchor" key carries the full locator (writeback target for RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR).
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"anchor\":\"${LOCATOR}\",\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\",\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\"}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing anchor results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Enshrine step complete ==="

71	==== Capture Composition Contract (rblds_ spine)
72	
73	This section governs the host-composed `projects.builds.create` path for Lode
74	captures — distinct from the trigger-autodetect path the Decision above covers.
75	A Lode capture is composed on the host by the capture-assembly spine
76	(`rblds_Spine.sh`) from two data inputs handed by a per-kind body (e.g. bole's
77	`zrbld_ensconce_submit`): an ordered _recipe_ of step rows and an opaque
78	_substitutions_ blob. The spine carries no per-kind branch and no
79	kind-registration mechanism — a new Lode kind is a new body file the spine
80	consumes as data, never a conditional inside the spine.
81	
82	===== Spine/body boundary
83	
84	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
85	|===
86	|Owner |Carries
87	
88	|Spine (`rblds_`)
89	|The step-composition loop (recipe rows -> `steps[]`); the Build-resource
90	envelope shape (`serviceAccount`, `options.automapSubstitutions`,
91	`options.logging`, `options.pool`, `timeout`); submit + build-id persist +
92	console link; the poll via `zrbfc_wait_build_completion`; and the
93	`buildStepOutputs[N]` base64-decode. The capture-domain constants — the mason
94	capture SA, the `TETHER` pool, the regime build timeout — are spine-owned,
95	because every Lode capture fetches upstream bytes as mason over the network.
96	
97	|Body (`rbld«kind»_`)
98	|The recipe rows (pre-resolved); the substitutions blob; the poll ceiling (the
99	one kind-flavored build knob); and the interpretation of the decoded step
100	output (the per-member envelope fan-out).
101	|===
102	
103	The only kind-flavored build knob the body passes is the poll ceiling (ensconce
104	borrows the enshrine ceiling). `serviceAccount`/`pool`/`timeout` are NOT body
105	parameters: they are capture-domain invariants the spine owns. A future kind
106	that needed a different pool or SA would change the spine's capture-domain
107	constants deliberately, not slip past as recipe data.
108	
109	===== Recipe row format
110	
111	A recipe is an ordered list of `|`-delimited step rows. The `|` delimiter is
112	load-bearing: builder image refs carry colons (tags and `sha256:` digests), so a
113	colon delimiter would be ambiguous.
114	
115	----
116	script_path | builder_image | id | entrypoint
117	----
118	
119	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
120	|===
121	|Field |Meaning
122	
123	|`script_path`   |Absolute path to the step script, _pre-resolved by the body_. The spine owns no steps-directory knowledge.
124	|`builder_image` |The step's builder image ref (a reliquary tool image).
125	|`id`            |The Cloud Build step id.
126	|`entrypoint`    |`bash` \| `sh` \| `python3` — selects the composed shebang line. Any other value fails composition.
127	|===
128	
129	The spine reads each script's body (shebang stripped), prepends the shebang the
130	entrypoint selects, and inlines it as the step's `script` field. This is the
131	same shape the made-side `rbfca_` assemblers use; the capture spine is the
132	canonical single copy for the fetched side.
133	
134	===== Substitutions blob — opaque
135	
136	The body composes the substitutions object (the `_RBGL_*` keys) into a file; the
137	spine slots it verbatim into the Build resource's `substitutions` field and
138	reads no key from it. `options.automapSubstitutions` maps the keys into each
139	step's environment. The spine's opacity to the blob is what keeps it
140	registration-agnostic — a new kind authors a different blob without touching the
141	spine.
142	
143	Invariant (RBSCB): substitutions and `/workspace` carry non-secret data only;
144	secrets are step-local and re-minted.
145	
146	===== Extract contract
147	
148	The spine's extract decodes one step's `buildStepOutputs[N]` slot — base64 to a
149	destination file — where `N` is the only generic parameter. The decoded JSON's
150	shape (for bole: `slot_1`..`slot_N` member envelopes, each a stamp + vouch) is
151	the body's knowledge; the spine is member-count-agnostic. This keeps the
152	N-member envelope axis out of the spine.
153	
154	===== Requires/provides format (designed here; enforced by the recipe validator)
155	
156	The recipe row above is the format implemented today. The capture domain also
157	needs a per-step _requires/provides_ declaration so a dispatch-time validator
158	can reject a bad composition before the expensive build. The FORMAT is fixed
159	here; its enforcement is the recipe-validator pace.
160	
161	A step composes over two register channels:
162	
163	* _Substitution registers_ — `_RBGL_*` keys present in the substitutions blob,
164	  automapped into the step environment.
165	* _/workspace registers_ — non-secret files a step writes for a later step,
166	  named by path relative to `/workspace`.
167	
168	Each step declares two token lists:
169	
170	* `requires` — substitution-register keys and `/workspace` register paths the
171	  step reads before it runs.
172	* `provides` — `/workspace` register paths the step writes (and, for the
173	  terminal capture step, the `buildStepOutputs` slot it authors).
174	
175	The validator walks the recipe in order: every `requires` token must be
176	satisfied by either a key present in the substitutions blob or a `provides`
177	token from an earlier step. An unsatisfiable `requires` fails dispatch with a
178	named-token diagnostic, before submit.
179	
180	The format is deliberately shaped by the capture domain's three axes:
181	
182	* _N-member envelopes_ — the terminal step's `provides` is the single
183	  `buildStepOutputs` slot, not per-member registers; member fan-out is body
184	  knowledge and stays out of the step contract.
185	* _Tool variety_ — registers are declared identically regardless of builder
186	  image or entrypoint, so a `python3` step and a `bash` step speak the same
187	  contract vocabulary.
188	* _Opaque-blob payloads_ — `requires` references substitution keys by NAME; the
189	  validator checks key _presence_, never value shape, preserving the spine's
190	  opacity to the blob.

file.content:
==== Capture Composition Contract (rblds_ spine)

This section governs the host-composed `projects.builds.create` path for Lode
captures — distinct from the trigger-autodetect path the Decision above covers.
A Lode capture is composed on the host by the capture-assembly spine
(`rblds_Spine.sh`) from two data inputs handed by a per-kind body (e.g. bole's
`zrbld_ensconce_submit`): an ordered _recipe_ of step rows and an opaque
_substitutions_ blob. The spine carries no per-kind branch and no
kind-registration mechanism — a new Lode kind is a new body file the spine
consumes as data, never a conditional inside the spine.

===== Spine/body boundary

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Owner |Carries

|Spine (`rblds_`)
|The step-composition loop (recipe rows -> `steps[]`); the Build-resource
envelope shape (`serviceAccount`, `options.automapSubstitutions`,
`options.logging`, `options.pool`, `timeout`); submit + build-id persist +
console link; the poll via `zrbfc_wait_build_completion`; and the
`buildStepOutputs[N]` base64-decode. The capture-domain constants — the mason
capture SA, the `TETHER` pool, the regime build timeout — are spine-owned,
because every Lode capture fetches upstream bytes as mason over the network.

|Body (`rbld«kind»_`)
|The recipe rows (pre-resolved); the substitutions blob; the poll ceiling (the
one kind-flavored build knob); and the interpretation of the decoded step
output (the per-member envelope fan-out).
|===

The only kind-flavored build knob the body passes is the poll ceiling (ensconce
borrows the enshrine ceiling). `serviceAccount`/`pool`/`timeout` are NOT body
parameters: they are capture-domain invariants the spine owns. A future kind
that needed a different pool or SA would change the spine's capture-domain
constants deliberately, not slip past as recipe data.

===== Recipe row format

A recipe is an ordered list of `|`-delimited step rows. The `|` delimiter is
load-bearing: builder image refs carry colons (tags and `sha256:` digests), so a
colon delimiter would be ambiguous.

----
script_path | builder_image | id | entrypoint
----

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Field |Meaning

|`script_path`   |Absolute path to the step script, _pre-resolved by the body_. The spine owns no steps-directory knowledge.
|`builder_image` |The step's builder image ref (a reliquary tool image).
|`id`            |The Cloud Build step id.
|`entrypoint`    |`bash` \| `sh` \| `python3` — selects the composed shebang line. Any other value fails composition.
|===

The spine reads each script's body (shebang stripped), prepends the shebang the
entrypoint selects, and inlines it as the step's `script` field. This is the
same shape the made-side `rbfca_` assemblers use; the capture spine is the
canonical single copy for the fetched side.

===== Substitutions blob — opaque

The body composes the substitutions object (the `_RBGL_*` keys) into a file; the
spine slots it verbatim into the Build resource's `substitutions` field and
reads no key from it. `options.automapSubstitutions` maps the keys into each
step's environment. The spine's opacity to the blob is what keeps it
registration-agnostic — a new kind authors a different blob without touching the
spine.

Invariant (RBSCB): substitutions and `/workspace` carry non-secret data only;
secrets are step-local and re-minted.

===== Extract contract

The spine's extract decodes one step's `buildStepOutputs[N]` slot — base64 to a
destination file — where `N` is the only generic parameter. The decoded JSON's
shape (for bole: `slot_1`..`slot_N` member envelopes, each a stamp + vouch) is
the body's knowledge; the spine is member-count-agnostic. This keeps the
N-member envelope axis out of the spine.

===== Requires/provides format (designed here; enforced by the recipe validator)

The recipe row above is the format implemented today. The capture domain also
needs a per-step _requires/provides_ declaration so a dispatch-time validator
can reject a bad composition before the expensive build. The FORMAT is fixed
here; its enforcement is the recipe-validator pace.

A step composes over two register channels:

* _Substitution registers_ — `_RBGL_*` keys present in the substitutions blob,
  automapped into the step environment.
* _/workspace registers_ — non-secret files a step writes for a later step,
  named by path relative to `/workspace`.

Each step declares two token lists:

* `requires` — substitution-register keys and `/workspace` register paths the
  step reads before it runs.
* `provides` — `/workspace` register paths the step writes (and, for the
  terminal capture step, the `buildStepOutputs` slot it authors).

The validator walks the recipe in order: every `requires` token must be
satisfied by either a key present in the substitutions blob or a `provides`
token from an earlier step. An unsatisfiable `requires` fails dispatch with a
named-token diagnostic, before submit.

The format is deliberately shaped by the capture domain's three axes:

* _N-member envelopes_ — the terminal step's `provides` is the single
  `buildStepOutputs` slot, not per-member registers; member fan-out is body
  knowledge and stays out of the step contract.
* _Tool variety_ — registers are declared identically regardless of builder
  image or entrypoint, so a `python3` step and a `bash` step speak the same
  contract vocabulary.
* _Opaque-blob payloads_ — `requires` references substitution keys by NAME; the
  validator checks key _presence_, never value shape, preserving the spine's
  opacity to the blob.

content.text: Confirmed the spine's contract: it inlines whole step files, the body owns the recipe. Now the pivotal integration question for the made-side touch — **how is `rbgjv03` (hallmark-verify) composed and inlined?** That determines where the shared vouch-push snippet machinery has to live so both the Lode spine *and* the made-side assembler can reach it. Let me trace it.

121	# Internal: assemble vouch step scripts into JSON array file
122	# Args: output_file temp_prefix
123	# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_* image refs from module state
124	zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps() {
125	  zrbfc_sentinel
126	
127	  local -r z_output_file="$1"
128	  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"
129	
130	  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
131	  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
132	  local -r z_vouch_step_defs=(
133	    "rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_alpine}|sh|prepare-keys"
134	    "rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|verify-provenance"
135	    "rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|assemble-push-vouch"
136	  )
137	
138	  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize vouch steps JSON"
139	
140	  local z_vdef=""
141	  local z_vscript=""
142	  local z_vbuilder=""
143	  local z_ventrypoint=""
144	  local z_vid=""
145	  local z_vscript_path=""
146	  local z_vbody_file=""
147	  local z_vescaped_file=""
148	  local z_vsteps_file=""
149	  local z_vbody=""
150	  local z_vshebang=""
151	
152	  for z_vdef in "${z_vouch_step_defs[@]}"; do
153	    IFS='|' read -r z_vscript z_vbuilder z_ventrypoint z_vid <<< "${z_vdef}"
154	    z_vscript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}/${z_vscript}"
155	    z_vbody_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_body.txt"
156	    z_vescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_escaped.txt"
157	    z_vsteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_steps.json"
158	
159	    test -f "${z_vscript_path}" || buc_die "Vouch step script not found: ${z_vscript_path}"
160	
161	    buc_log_args "Reading script body for ${z_vid} (skip shebang)"
162	    zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_vscript_path}" "${z_vbody_file}" \
163	      || buc_die "Failed to read vouch step script: ${z_vscript_path}"
164	    z_vbody=$(<"${z_vbody_file}")
165	    test -n "${z_vbody}" || buc_die "Empty vouch script body: ${z_vscript_path}"
166	
167	    case "${z_ventrypoint}" in
168	      bash)    z_vshebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
169	      sh)      z_vshebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
170	      python3) z_vshebang="#!/usr/bin/env python3" ;;
171	      *)       buc_die "Unknown entrypoint: ${z_ventrypoint}" ;;
172	    esac
173	    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_vshebang}" "${z_vbody}" > "${z_vescaped_file}" \
174	      || buc_die "Failed to write vouch script body for ${z_vid}"
175	
176	    buc_log_args "Appending vouch step ${z_vid} to JSON array"
177	    jq \
178	      --arg name "${z_vbuilder}" \
179	      --arg id "${z_vid}" \
180	      --rawfile script "${z_vescaped_file}" \
181	      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
182	      "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vsteps_file}" \
183	      || buc_die "Failed to append vouch step ${z_vid} to JSON"
184	    mv "${z_vsteps_file}" "${z_output_file}" \
185	      || buc_die "Failed to update vouch steps JSON for ${z_vid}"
186	  done
187	}
188	
189	# Internal: assemble single-step preflight JSON array file
190	# Args: output_file temp_prefix
191	# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_alpine from module state.
192	# Single step prepends to every ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure, bind,
193	# graft, enshrine) as defense-in-depth: validates reliquary GAR-presence from
194	# the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
195	zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step() {
196	  zrbfc_sentinel
197	
198	  local -r z_output_file="$1"
199	  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"
200	
201	  local -r z_pscript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh"
202	  local -r z_pbody_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_body.txt"
203	  local -r z_pescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_escaped.txt"
204	  local -r z_psteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_steps.json"
205	
206	  test -f "${z_pscript_path}" || buc_die "Preflight step script not found: ${z_pscript_path}"
207	
208	  buc_log_args "Reading preflight step script (skip shebang)"
209	  zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_pscript_path}" "${z_pbody_file}" \
210	    || buc_die "Failed to read preflight step script: ${z_pscript_path}"
211	  local z_pbody=""
212	  z_pbody=$(<"${z_pbody_file}")
213	  test -n "${z_pbody}" || buc_die "Empty preflight script body"
214	
215	  printf '#!/bin/sh\n%s' "${z_pbody}" > "${z_pescaped_file}" \
216	    || buc_die "Failed to write escaped preflight script body"
217	
218	  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize preflight steps JSON"
219	  jq \
220	    --arg name "${z_rbfc_tool_alpine}" \
221	    --arg id "reliquary-preflight" \
222	    --rawfile script "${z_pescaped_file}" \
223	    '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \

    mv "${z_asteps_file}" "${z_output_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to update about steps JSON for ${z_aid}"

}

# Internal: assemble vouch step scripts into JSON array file
# Args: output_file temp_prefix
# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_* image refs from module state
zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_output_file="$1"
  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"

  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
  local -r z_vouch_step_defs=(
    "rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_alpine}|sh|prepare-keys"
    "rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|verify-provenance"
    "rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|assemble-push-vouch"
  )

  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize vouch steps JSON"

  local z_vdef=""
  local z_vscript=""
  local z_vbuilder=""
  local z_ventrypoint=""
  local z_vid=""
  local z_vscript_path=""
  local z_vbody_file=""
  local z_vescaped_file=""
  local z_vsteps_file=""
  local z_vbody=""
  local z_vshebang=""

  for z_vdef in "${z_vouch_step_defs[@]}"; do
    IFS='|' read -r z_vscript z_vbuilder z_ventrypoint z_vid <<< "${z_vdef}"
    z_vscript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}/${z_vscript}"
    z_vbody_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_body.txt"
    z_vescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_escaped.txt"
    z_vsteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_steps.json"

    test -f "${z_vscript_path}" || buc_die "Vouch step script not found: ${z_vscript_path}"

    buc_log_args "Reading script body for ${z_vid} (skip shebang)"
    zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_vscript_path}" "${z_vbody_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to read vouch step script: ${z_vscript_path}"
    z_vbody=$(<"${z_vbody_file}")
    test -n "${z_vbody}" || buc_die "Empty vouch script body: ${z_vscript_path}"

    case "${z_ventrypoint}" in
      bash)    z_vshebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
      sh)      z_vshebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
      python3) z_vshebang="#!/usr/bin/env python3" ;;
      *)       buc_die "Unknown entrypoint: ${z_ventrypoint}" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_vshebang}" "${z_vbody}" > "${z_vescaped_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write vouch script body for ${z_vid}"

    buc_log_args "Appending vouch step ${z_vid} to JSON array"
    jq \
      --arg name "${z_vbuilder}" \
      --arg id "${z_vid}" \
      --rawfile script "${z_vescaped_file}" \
      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
      "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vsteps_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to append vouch step ${z_vid} to JSON"
    mv "${z_vsteps_file}" "${z_output_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to update vouch steps JSON for ${z_vid}"
  done
}

# Internal: assemble single-step preflight JSON array file
# Args: output_file temp_prefix
# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_alpine from module state.
# Single step prepends to every ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure, bind,
# graft, enshrine) as defense-in-depth: validates reliquary GAR-presence from
# the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_output_file="$1"
  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"

  local -r z_pscript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh"
  local -r z_pbody_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_body.txt"
  local -r z_pescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_escaped.txt"
  local -r z_psteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_steps.json"

  test -f "${z_pscript_path}" || buc_die "Preflight step script not found: ${z_pscript_path}"

  buc_log_args "Reading preflight step script (skip shebang)"
  zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_pscript_path}" "${z_pbody_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read preflight step script: ${z_pscript_path}"
  local z_pbody=""
  z_pbody=$(<"${z_pbody_file}")
  test -n "${z_pbody}" || buc_die "Empty preflight script body"

  printf '#!/bin/sh\n%s' "${z_pbody}" > "${z_pescaped_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write escaped preflight script body"

  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize preflight steps JSON"
  jq \
    --arg name "${z_rbfc_tool_alpine}" \
    --arg id "reliquary-preflight" \
    --rawfile script "${z_pescaped_file}" \
    '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
    "${z_output_file}" > "${z_psteps_file}" \

content.text: I now have the complete mechanism and the made-side path. Both the Lode spine and the made-side vouch assembler (`zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps`) inline steps the same way — read body via `zrbfc_write_script_body`, prepend shebang, jq-append. That tells me exactly where the shared snippet machinery has to sit. Let me lock the design and check the one remaining unknown — how cloud step scripts get shellchecked, since snippet *fragments* (no shebang, externally-set vars) will fight the linter.

=== shellcheckrc ===
# busc_shellcheckrc — BCG-structural shellcheck suppressions
#
# These codes are false positives caused by BCG's module architecture:
# dynamic sourcing via BURD_* paths, cross-module kindle/literal constants,
# and indirect function invocation via source/dispatch.
#
# Policy: No inline shellcheck directives anywhere in the codebase.
# All structural suppressions live here. Genuine findings must be fixed.
#
# Companion: BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md § Shellcheck Integration

# SC1090: Can't follow non-constant source.
#   BCG furnish pattern sources via "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/module.sh" —
#   shellcheck cannot resolve variable-based paths.
disable=SC1090

# SC1091: Not following: file was not specified as input.
#   Same root cause as SC1090. Shellcheck doesn't know sourced files
#   exist because paths are computed at runtime.
disable=SC1091

# SC2034: Variable appears unused.
#   Literal constants and kindle constants are defined in one module
#   and consumed by other modules after sourcing. Shellcheck sees
#   assignment but not the cross-module usage.
disable=SC2034

# SC2154: Variable referenced but not assigned.
#   Inverse of SC2034. Variables defined in sourced modules (kindle
#   constants, literal constants, regime fields) are invisible to
#   shellcheck in the consuming file.
disable=SC2154

# SC2155: Declare and assign separately to avoid masking return values.
#   BCG explicitly blesses single-line $(<file) assignment:
#     local z_content=$(<"${Z_FILE}")
#   This is a bash builtin, not command substitution. BCG requires the
#   two-line pattern only for _capture functions using $().
disable=SC2155

# SC2153: Possible misspelling.
#   Cross-module variable names with similar prefixes (e.g., ZRBGU_PREFIX
#   used in rbgb module) trigger false misspelling suggestions.
disable=SC2153

# SC2329: Function is never invoked.
#   BUK functions are invoked indirectly via source and dispatch.
#   Shellcheck cannot trace the dynamic call graph.
disable=SC2329

# SC2016: Expressions don't expand in single quotes.
#   BCG template-generation patterns (buut_tabtarget.sh, buq_qualify.sh,
#   cloud build scripts) echo single-quoted strings containing ${} into
#   generated files (tabtargets, launchers, Dockerfiles). The expressions
#   are intended for the generated file's runtime, not the generator's.
disable=SC2016

# SC2254: Quote expansions in case patterns to match literally.
#   BCG glob-matching patterns intentionally use unquoted variables in
#   case statements (e.g., exemption patterns in buq_qualify.sh).
#   The glob expansion is the desired behavior.
disable=SC2254

# SC2059: Don't use variables in the printf format string.
#   BCG table-formatting patterns define a local -r format constant
#   (e.g., z_row_fmt) used across header, separator, and data rows.
#   The variable is always a locally-defined constant, never user input.
disable=SC2059

# ─── Site-specific false positives (verified, not BCG-structural) ───
# Isolated single-site false positives confirmed by reading the code.
# Disabling tree-wide is the accepted cost of the no-inline-directives policy.

# SC2088: Tilde does not expand in quotes.
#   jjfp_fundus.sh zjjfp_double_hop_pipe passes "~/.ssh/authorized_keys" through a
#   double-hop ssh pipe; the tilde stays literal locally and on the middle hop and
#   expands on the final remote shell to the target account's home. Local expansion
#   (curia operator's home) would be wrong — the quoting is correct.
disable=SC2088

# SC1003: Want to escape a single quote?
#   rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh Rule 3 uses the case glob *'\') to match a FROM line
#   ending in a literal backslash (Dockerfile line-continuation). The single-quoted
#   backslash is genuinely literal; no single-quote escaping is intended.
disable=SC1003

content.text: Key finding: **inline shellcheck directives are banned project-wide**, and SC2034 (assigned-unused) + SC2154 (used-unassigned) are *globally* disabled — which is exactly the fragment pattern (a snippet's `provides` are assigned-unused, its `requires` are used-unassigned). So snippets pass lint without inline directives. But I need to know how `buq_shellcheck` selects files and handles a missing shebang. Reading the selector.

183	buq_shellcheck() {
184	  local z_tools_dir="${1:-${BURD_TOOLS_DIR:-}}"
185	  local z_rcfile="${2:-${BURD_BUK_DIR:+${BURD_BUK_DIR}/busc_shellcheckrc}}"
186	  local z_result_file="${3:-${BURD_TEMP_DIR:+${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/buq_shellcheck_results.txt}}"
187	  test -n "${z_tools_dir}"   || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: tools directory required"
188	  test -n "${z_rcfile}"      || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: rcfile path required"
189	  test -n "${z_result_file}" || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: result file path required"
190	
191	  buc_step "Running shellcheck qualification"
192	
193	  test -f "${z_rcfile}"    || buc_die "Shellcheck rcfile not found: ${z_rcfile}"
194	  test -d "${z_tools_dir}" || buc_die "Tools directory not found: ${z_tools_dir}"
195	
196	  command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "shellcheck not found — install from https://www.shellcheck.net"
197	
198	  # Hard version pin — fail instantly if this station's shellcheck is not the
199	  # pinned version, before any check runs. Prevents silent cross-station
200	  # divergence (e.g. a pre-0.10.0 binary that rejects --rcfile). Keep stations
201	  # in lockstep; bump BUQ_SHELLCHECK_VERSION only after upgrading all of them.
202	  local z_sc_version=""
203	  z_sc_version="$(shellcheck --version | awk '/^version:/ {print $2}')"
204	  test "${z_sc_version}" = "${BUQ_SHELLCHECK_VERSION}" || buc_die \
205	    "shellcheck version mismatch: found '${z_sc_version}', require '${BUQ_SHELLCHECK_VERSION}'. Install the pinned version ahead of the system binary on PATH — static release: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/releases/tag/v${BUQ_SHELLCHECK_VERSION}"
206	
207	  # Collect .sh files (load-then-iterate per BCG)
208	  local z_files=()
209	  local z_file=""
210	  while IFS= read -r z_file || test -n "${z_file}"; do
211	    z_files+=("${z_file}")
212	  done < <(find "${z_tools_dir}" -name '*.sh' -type f | sort)
213	
214	  local -r z_file_count=${#z_files[@]}
215	  buc_log_args "Found ${z_file_count} shell files under ${z_tools_dir}"
216	
217	  test "${z_file_count}" -gt 0 || buc_die "No .sh files found under ${z_tools_dir}"
218	
219	  # Run shellcheck — capture output to temp file for forensics
220	  local z_status=0
221	  shellcheck --rcfile="${z_rcfile}" -S style -f gcc "${z_files[@]}" \
222	    > "${z_result_file}" 2>&1 \
223	    || z_status=$?
224	
225	  if test "${z_status}" = "0"; then
226	    buc_step "Shellcheck qualification passed: ${z_file_count} files clean"
227	    return 0
228	  fi
229	
230	  # Count and display findings
231	  local z_finding_count=0
232	  local z_discard
233	  while IFS= read -r z_discard || test -n "${z_discard:-}"; do
234	    z_finding_count=$((z_finding_count + 1))
235	  done < "${z_result_file}"
236	  buc_log_args "Shellcheck findings: ${z_finding_count} (see ${z_result_file})"
237	
238	  local z_line=""
239	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
240	    buc_warn "${z_line}"
241	  done < "${z_result_file}"
242	
243	  buc_die "Shellcheck qualification failed: ${z_finding_count} findings across ${z_file_count} files"

file.content:
buq_shellcheck() {
  local z_tools_dir="${1:-${BURD_TOOLS_DIR:-}}"
  local z_rcfile="${2:-${BURD_BUK_DIR:+${BURD_BUK_DIR}/busc_shellcheckrc}}"
  local z_result_file="${3:-${BURD_TEMP_DIR:+${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/buq_shellcheck_results.txt}}"
  test -n "${z_tools_dir}"   || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: tools directory required"
  test -n "${z_rcfile}"      || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: rcfile path required"
  test -n "${z_result_file}" || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: result file path required"

  buc_step "Running shellcheck qualification"

  test -f "${z_rcfile}"    || buc_die "Shellcheck rcfile not found: ${z_rcfile}"
  test -d "${z_tools_dir}" || buc_die "Tools directory not found: ${z_tools_dir}"

  command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "shellcheck not found — install from https://www.shellcheck.net"

  # Hard version pin — fail instantly if this station's shellcheck is not the
  # pinned version, before any check runs. Prevents silent cross-station
  # divergence (e.g. a pre-0.10.0 binary that rejects --rcfile). Keep stations
  # in lockstep; bump BUQ_SHELLCHECK_VERSION only after upgrading all of them.
  local z_sc_version=""
  z_sc_version="$(shellcheck --version | awk '/^version:/ {print $2}')"
  test "${z_sc_version}" = "${BUQ_SHELLCHECK_VERSION}" || buc_die \
    "shellcheck version mismatch: found '${z_sc_version}', require '${BUQ_SHELLCHECK_VERSION}'. Install the pinned version ahead of the system binary on PATH — static release: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/releases/tag/v${BUQ_SHELLCHECK_VERSION}"

  # Collect .sh files (load-then-iterate per BCG)
  local z_files=()
  local z_file=""
  while IFS= read -r z_file || test -n "${z_file}"; do
    z_files+=("${z_file}")
  done < <(find "${z_tools_dir}" -name '*.sh' -type f | sort)

  local -r z_file_count=${#z_files[@]}
  buc_log_args "Found ${z_file_count} shell files under ${z_tools_dir}"

  test "${z_file_count}" -gt 0 || buc_die "No .sh files found under ${z_tools_dir}"

  # Run shellcheck — capture output to temp file for forensics
  local z_status=0
  shellcheck --rcfile="${z_rcfile}" -S style -f gcc "${z_files[@]}" \
    > "${z_result_file}" 2>&1 \
    || z_status=$?

  if test "${z_status}" = "0"; then
    buc_step "Shellcheck qualification passed: ${z_file_count} files clean"
    return 0
  fi

  # Count and display findings
  local z_finding_count=0
  local z_discard
  while IFS= read -r z_discard || test -n "${z_discard:-}"; do
    z_finding_count=$((z_finding_count + 1))
  done < "${z_result_file}"
  buc_log_args "Shellcheck findings: ${z_finding_count} (see ${z_result_file})"

  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    buc_warn "${z_line}"
  done < "${z_result_file}"

  buc_die "Shellcheck qualification failed: ${z_finding_count} findings across ${z_file_count} files"
}

The design is locked. Let me track it as tasks so you can follow the autonomous push.

Paused. Nothing's been written yet — I'm at the design-locked / about-to-implement boundary. Here's exactly where I am so you can redirect:

**Decisions made (with your "do the right thing" grant):**
1. **Vouch-push** → share the *primitive*, not the whole step (the cinch revision you accepted). Confirmed against bytes: only a 3-line buildx bootstrap is verbatim; cardinality/URI/context stay per-kind.
2. **Skopeo snippet** → I narrowed it from the docket's guess. The docket guessed "inspect→digest→fingerprint→**first-copy**, with destination URI as a parameter." But both kinds' first-copy *destination* is SHA-derived (computed inside the block), so it can't be a clean pre-set parameter. The lossless shared atom is **inspect→digest→fingerprint** only; the copies stay per-kind. This is a refinement against the bytes, exactly what the docket's open item invited.
3. **Four snippets, not three**: `token-fetch`, `skopeo-fingerprint`, `buildx-bootstrap`, `buildx-push` (I split bootstrap from push so the run-once bootstrap and per-artifact push have clean separate contracts).
4. **Mechanism**: snippets are cloud fragments in a new `Tools/rbk/rbgjs/` home; an `#@rbgjs_include <name>` marker is spliced at host-assembly time by a new **additive** `zrbfc_expand_includes` in `rbfcb_BuildHost.sh` (sibling to `write_script_body`, no existing logic touched), reached by both the Lode spine and the made-side vouch assembler. Snippets read **shell vars** the kind sets before the include — never `_RBGx_` substitutions — which is what lets one snippet serve `_RBGL_*` and `_RBGV_*` callers.
5. **Blast radius kept surgical**: the only made-side edit is one `expand` call in `zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps` (where v03 lives) — the one touch the paddock sanctions.

**The one thing brushing a cinch:** decision #4 adds a function to a Foundry-core file (`rbfcb_`). That's additive (new function + new const, zero existing-logic edits), and the "Foundry relocation-only" cinch governs the *explosion* paces, not this library pace — so I judged it in-bounds. Flagging it because it's the closest call.

223	# Internal: normalize a path argument for a Windows-native build tool (docker
224	# under Cygwin). A Windows-native binary reads a Cygwin /cygdrive/X/... path as a
225	# literal Windows path and reports "does not exist"; hand it the drive-letter
226	# form (X:/... — forward slashes, which Windows accepts). Pure /cygdrive
227	# parameter expansion, no cygpath, mirroring RBTDRX's fast path and zrbte_kindle.
228	# Gated on BURD_OSTYPE (the dispatch-synthesized platform fact): off Cygwin every
229	# path is emitted unchanged. A relative or already-native path passes through; a
230	# bare-absolute POSIX path (leading / but not /cygdrive) is an unsurveyed shape
231	# and returns 1 so the caller dies. Kindle-independent — reads only its argument
232	# and BURD_OSTYPE — so, like zrbfc_write_script_body, it carries no sentinel and
233	# stays unit-testable without the foundry kindle chain. Retire when the foundry

z_url=$(<"${z_url_file}")
  test -n "${z_url}" || buc_die "Empty git repo URL from ${z_url_file}"
  local z_repo="${z_url#*://*/}"
  z_repo="${z_repo%.git}"
  echo "${z_repo}" > "${ZRBFC_GIT_REPO_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write derived git repo"
}

# Internal: write a script's body (everything after the shebang line) to a file
# using only builtins — the portable replacement for `tail -n +2 src > dst`.
# Returns non-zero if the source is unreadable or the destination unwritable.
zrbfc_write_script_body() {
  local -r z_src="$1"
  local -r z_dst="$2"
  local z_line
  local z_seen_shebang=""
  test -r "${z_src}" || return 1
  : > "${z_dst}"     || return 1
  while IFS= read -r z_line || [ -n "${z_line}" ]; do
    if [ -z "${z_seen_shebang}" ]; then
      z_seen_shebang=1
      continue
    fi
    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" >> "${z_dst}"
  done < "${z_src}"
  return 0
}

# Internal: normalize a path argument for a Windows-native build tool (docker
# under Cygwin). A Windows-native binary reads a Cygwin /cygdrive/X/... path as a
# literal Windows path and reports "does not exist"; hand it the drive-letter
# form (X:/... — forward slashes, which Windows accepts). Pure /cygdrive
# parameter expansion, no cygpath, mirroring RBTDRX's fast path and zrbte_kindle.
# Gated on BURD_OSTYPE (the dispatch-synthesized platform fact): off Cygwin every
# path is emitted unchanged. A relative or already-native path passes through; a
# bare-absolute POSIX path (leading / but not /cygdrive) is an unsurveyed shape
# and returns 1 so the caller dies. Kindle-independent — reads only its argument
# and BURD_OSTYPE — so, like zrbfc_write_script_body, it carries no sentinel and
# stays unit-testable without the foundry kindle chain. Retire when the foundry
# builds as a true Cygwin binary.

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS snippet: OAuth2 access token from the Cloud Build metadata server (Mason
# SA). Composed, not standalone — spliced into a step via
#   #@rbgjs_include token-fetch
# The shebang exists only so shellcheck lints this fragment as bash; the include
# expander strips it on splice. The fragment runs inside the kind's step body,
# which owns `set -euo pipefail`.
#
# The metadata-server token is the canonical skopeo-to-GAR authentication
# (RBSCB): skopeo cannot use the gcloud credential helper, so the token is
# fetched in-memory and never written to /workspace — which carries non-secret
# data only.
#
#   requires: (none — ambient metadata server, parameter-free)
#   provides: TOKEN  (shell var — OAuth2 access token)
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS snippet: inspect an upstream image's raw manifest, take its canonical
# sha256 digest, and derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint. Composed,
# not standalone — spliced into a step via
#   #@rbgjs_include skopeo-fingerprint
# The shebang exists only so shellcheck lints this fragment as bash; the include
# expander strips it on splice.
#
# The canonical digest is the sha256 of the RAW manifest bytes — what every tool
# reports for the image. The fingerprint is the sanitized origin (`:` and `/`
# become `-`) plus the first 10 hex of that digest, the legacy enshrine anchor
# form. Only this inspect+digest+fingerprint core is byte-identical across the
# skopeo captures (ensconce, enshrine); the first GAR copy and any retag/envelope
# are kind-specific (their destination tags are themselves digest-derived) and
# stay in the step, so only this core is shared.
#
#   requires: ORIGIN    (shell var — upstream image ref, e.g. docker.io/library/x:tag)
#             RAW_FILE  (shell var — /workspace path to write the raw manifest into)
#   provides: SHA          (shell var — 64-hex canonical digest, no algorithm prefix)
#             FINGERPRINT  (shell var — "<sanitized-origin>-<first-10-hex>")
skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

# sha256sum (coreutils, not openssl) — runs inside the skopeo reliquary container.
SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

# Sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars (legacy anchor form).
FINGERPRINT="$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')-${SHA:0:10}"

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS snippet: ensure the shared docker-container buildx builder exists and is
# selected. Composed, not standalone — spliced into a step via
#   #@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap
# The shebang exists only so shellcheck lints this fragment as bash; the include
# expander strips it on splice.
#
# Idempotent under Cloud Build retry: inspect-or-create, then use. Run once per
# step before any push; safe to re-run (a second inspect succeeds, create is
# skipped, use is a no-op).
#
#   requires: (none)
#   provides: the "rb-builder" docker-container buildx builder, created if absent
#             and selected as the active builder
docker buildx inspect rb-builder >/dev/null 2>&1 \
  || docker buildx create --driver docker-container --name rb-builder
docker buildx use rb-builder

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJS snippet: push a prepared FROM-scratch build context as an image via the
# shared buildx builder. Composed, not standalone — spliced into a step via
#   #@rbgjs_include buildx-push
# The shebang exists only so shellcheck lints this fragment as bash; the include
# expander strips it on splice.
#
# This is the irreducible push shared by the FROM-scratch vouch-push callers
# (Lode rbgjl02, hallmark-verify rbgjv03). Everything kind-specific — how many
# times you push (Lode loops N members; verify pushes once), the destination URI,
# the platform set (Lode pins linux/amd64; verify reads a platforms file), and
# the context assembly (which JSON, conditional Dockerfile) — is done by the kind
# before the include. Requires the builder from buildx-bootstrap.
#
#   requires: PUSH_URI        (shell var — full destination image ref including tag)
#             PUSH_PLATFORMS  (shell var — buildx --platform value, e.g. linux/amd64)
#             PUSH_CTX        (shell var — build context dir holding the Dockerfile)
#             (the rb-builder builder — see buildx-bootstrap)
#   provides: the image pushed to PUSH_URI
docker buildx build \
  --push \
  --platform="${PUSH_PLATFORMS}" \
  --tag "${PUSH_URI}" \
  "${PUSH_CTX}" \
  || { echo "FATAL: buildx push failed for ${PUSH_URI}" >&2; exit 1; }
